findings.title: N+1 Query Problem in User Search
findings.description: The search endpoint fetches users in one query, then loops through each user to fetch their profile individually. This creates N+1 database queries which will cause severe performance degradation as the user base grows.
findings.suggestion: Use a JOIN query or batch loading to fetch all profiles in a single query: `SELECT u.*, p.bio, p.avatar_url FROM users u LEFT JOIN user_profiles p ON u.id = p.user_id WHERE ...`
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Clear N+1 pattern visible in diff. The loop at lines 49-55 executes a separate query for each user. Context shows db.ts has transaction() helper available but not used.

findings.title: Missing Database Index on Email Column
findings.description: The search query uses LIKE on the email column without an index. This will result in full table scans and slow query performance as the users table grows.
findings.suggestion: Add a database index on the email column: `CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);` or use a full-text search solution for better performance with LIKE queries.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: LIKE query on email column visible in diff. No migration file or index creation in the PR. This is a legitimate performance concern for production.

findings.title: Missing Accessibility Attributes on Search Input
findings.description: The search input lacks proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support. Screen reader users won't know what the input is for, and keyboard-only users can't navigate the results effectively.
findings.code_snippet:
<input
        type="text"
        placeholder="Search by email"
        value={filters.email}
        onChange={(e) => setFilters({ ...filters, email: e.target.value })}
      />
findings.suggestion: Add aria-label="Search users by email" to the input, implement keyboard navigation (arrow keys) for results, and add role="search" to the container div.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Input element in diff has no aria-label, aria-describedby, or role attributes. Context shows UserProfile.tsx has proper accessibility (aria-label on role badge), making this inconsistency more notable.

findings.title: Inconsistent Error Handling and Missing Logging
findings.description: The error handling returns a generic 500 status for all errors without distinguishing between client errors (400) and server errors (500). Additionally, there's no error logging which makes debugging production issues difficult.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper error classification: return 400 for validation errors, 500 for server errors. Add structured logging: `logger.error('Search failed', { error, filters: req.query });`
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Generic catch block with 500 status visible in diff. No error logging, no error type checking. The existing /users/:id endpoint (visible in diff context) has similar issues, showing a pattern of poor error handling.

findings.title: Missing Edge Case Tests for Search Feature
findings.description: The new search endpoint lacks tests for critical edge cases: empty results, special characters in search terms, pagination boundaries, and concurrent requests. This increases the risk of bugs in production.
findings.code_snippet:
// New search endpoint with filters
router.get('/users/search', async (req, res) => {
findings.suggestion: Add test cases for: 1) Empty search results, 2) SQL injection attempts via search params, 3) Page number edge cases (0, negative, beyond max), 4) Special characters (%, _, \), 5) Concurrent search requests.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: No test files added in this PR (diff shows only src/api/users.ts, src/components/UserSearch.tsx, src/db.ts). Package.json shows jest is available but not used for this feature.

findings.title: API Documentation Missing New Filter Parameters
findings.description: The API documentation doesn't mention the new filter parameters (email, name, role, page) added to the search endpoint. This will confuse API consumers and lead to support requests.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc comments documenting all query parameters, their types, and examples: `@param {string} [email] - Filter by email (partial match)` or update OpenAPI/Swagger spec.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Only a basic comment '// New search endpoint with filters' visible in diff. No JSDoc, no parameter documentation. The existing /users/:id endpoint also lacks documentation, showing a pattern.

findings.title: Magic Number for Page Size
findings.description: The page size is hardcoded as 20 in multiple places without using a named constant. This makes it difficult to change the page size consistently and reduces code maintainability.
findings.suggestion: Define a constant at the top of the file: `const DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 20;` and use it in both places. Consider making it configurable via environment variable.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Number 20 appears twice in lines 43-44 of diff without a named constant. This is a minor but legitimate code quality issue.

findings.title: Inconsistent Naming Convention
findings.description: The code mixes camelCase (setResults, setFilters) and snake_case (set_loading) naming conventions in the same component. This violates JavaScript/TypeScript conventions and reduces code readability.
findings.suggestion: Rename `set_loading` to `setLoading` to match JavaScript/TypeScript conventions and maintain consistency with other state setters in the component.
findings.validation_notes: Confirmed: Line 16 shows 'set_loading' (snake_case) while line 15 shows 'setResults' (camelCase) in the same component. Clear inconsistency visible in diff.
